findings: Independent verification confirms the species, current scientific name, family, all three measurements, short chisel-like bill, striped head, white back stripe, spotted wings, black-spotted white outer tail feathers, and male red nape.

The illustration agrees closely with the reference photos: compact body, short bill, dark eye, male nape patch, checkered wing, stiff pointed tail, and zygodactyl feet are all plausibly rendered. One wing is visible in profile; the second is naturally occluded. The bird otherwise has one head, one beak, two legs, and one tail.
All visible plate text is factually supported. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
No material species, anatomy, or text errors found. Minor stylization in feather/leg detail does not compromise identification or scientific readability.
